question.text: Which of the following statements about PPF is INCORRECT?

answer.text: PPF interest rate is linked to G-sec rates and varies quarterly
PPF allows partial withdrawal any time after opening the account

explanation.text: **The Logic:** PPF allows partial withdrawal only after completion of 5 years from the end of the financial year in which the initial subscription was made -- NOT at any time. All other statements are correct: PPF has a 15-year lock-in, its interest rate varies quarterly linked to G-sec rates, and it enjoys EEE tax treatment. **The Trap:** Candidates who know PPF permits partial withdrawal may overlook the 5-year waiting period condition and assume it is available anytime, making this incorrect statement appear correct. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A covers PPF features; NISM Series V-A touches on tax-saving instruments comparison as part of the suitability module. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 5-year partial withdrawal restriction in PPF means clients needing interim liquidity before Year 6 should complement PPF with a more liquid instrument like a short-duration debt fund or liquid fund for their emergency needs.
